message:
test: borrow fixes, explicit format args, and a missing io::Write import

Five small, mechanical fixes to test files so they compile cleanly
under the current rustc / clippy configuration. No production code
changes; no test semantics changed.

- tests/proptest_error_renderer.rs — `prop_assert_eq!` was comparing
  `parsed["category"]` (which returns a `&Value`) against a
  freshly-constructed `expected: Value`, relying on an implicit auto-
  ref that rustc no longer performs here after a macro expansion
  change. Take explicit references to match the macro's expected
  `&T: PartialEq<&T>` binding: `&parsed["category"]` vs `&expected`.
  Applied in both the top-level property and the
  `render_error_code`-focused property.

- tests/proptest_outcome.rs — `CancelState::message` is `Option<String>`.
  The property was asserting `prop_assert_eq!(user.message, Some(message))`
  where `message: &str`, which no longer coerces across the generic
  bound. Switches to `user.message.as_deref()` to compare against
  `Some(message): Option<&str>` for each of `cancel_user`,
  `cancel_timeout`, and `cancel_shutdown`.

- tests/proptest_patterns_metamorphic.rs — the MR3 and two MR-chunk
  assertions used captured-format-arg syntax inside the
  `prop_assert_eq!` message while also passing `bytes.len()` /
  `chunks.len()` as positional args. proptest's macro inserts its own
  trailing arg, and the resulting `format_args!` arity no longer
  matched. Dropping the captured args for `batch_counts`,
  `chunked_counts`, `perm`, `canonical_ids`, `permuted_ids` and
  passing them positionally restores consistent formatting under the
  proptest macro expansion.

- tests/conformance_mcp_manifest.rs — relax
  `ResultExt::unwrap_err_or_else` so its closure argument is
  `impl FnOnce()` rather than `impl FnOnce() -> !`. The `-> !` bound
  required every caller to put `panic!` or similar inside the closure,
  which was brittle and drove a rustc "never type fallback" warning.
  The new impl invokes `f()` and then
  `panic!("expected error-producing branch to diverge")` if the input
  was `Ok`, so the trait still provides the diverging guarantee
  externally without forcing every call site to do so.

- tests/vector_index_tests.rs — add `use std::io::Write;` so the file
  can call `.flush()` / `.write_all(...)` on a `File` returned from a
  helper. Previously the trait method was visible transitively and
  is now not in scope, so the test failed to compile against newer
  nightly toolchains.

files.patch:
@@ -535,8 +535,10 @@ proptest! {
             &batch_counts,
             &chunked_counts,
             "MR chunk-vs-batch (compression=on) violated: \
-             batch {batch_counts:?} != chunked {chunked_counts:?}, \
+             batch {:?} != chunked {:?}, \
              bytes_len={}, n_chunks={}",
+            batch_counts,
+            chunked_counts,
             bytes.len(),
             chunks.len(),
         );
@@ -576,8 +578,10 @@ proptest! {
             &batch_counts,
             &chunked_counts,
             "MR chunk-vs-batch (compression=off) violated: \
-             batch {batch_counts:?} != chunked {chunked_counts:?}, \
+             batch {:?} != chunked {:?}, \
              bytes_len={}, n_chunks={}",
+            batch_counts,
+            chunked_counts,
             bytes.len(),
             chunks.len(),
         );
@@ -658,8 +662,11 @@ proptest! {
         prop_assert_eq!(
             &canonical_ids,
             &permuted_ids,
-            "MR3 violated: rule permutation {perm:?} changed detection set \
-             from {canonical_ids:?} to {permuted_ids:?} on text of length {}",
+            "MR3 violated: rule permutation {:?} changed detection set \
+             from {:?} to {:?} on text of length {}",
+            perm,
+            canonical_ids,
+            permuted_ids,
             text.len(),
         );
     }
